mission.title: linkerd: Can't deploy Linkerd v2.13.x with ArgoCD
mission.description: Can't deploy Linkerd v2.13.x with ArgoCD. This issue affects 26+ users.

steps.title: Identify linkerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `output, etc`

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
### What is the issue?

A Kubernetes Lease object is part of the Linkerd helm chart. This type of object is excluded by Argo, and thus the Lease is never created. This causes the `destination` pods to

steps.title: Apply the fix for Can't deploy Linkerd v2.13.x with ArgoCD
steps.description:
The Linkerd control plane chart contains a Lease resource which is used by the Policy controller to do leader election.  ArgoCD considers Leases to be runtime resources and will not deploy them.  This means that Linkerd will not work for users of ArgoCD.

We remove the policy-controller-write Lease 
```yaml
Error: failed to get lease: ApiError: leases.coordination.k8s.io "policy-controller-write" not found: NotFound (ErrorResponse { status: "Failure", message: "leases.coordination.k8s.io \"policy-controller-write\" not found", reason: "NotFound", code: 404 })


Caused by:
 0: ApiError: leases.coordination.k8s.io "policy-controller-write" not found: NotFound (ErrorResponse { status: "Failure", message: "leases.coordination.k8s.io \"policy-controller-write\" not found", reason: "NotFound", code: 404 })
 1: leases.coordination.k8s.io "policy-controller-write" not found: NotFound
```

steps.title: Confirm Can't deploy Linkerd v2.13.x with ArgoCD is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `output, etc` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: The Linkerd control plane chart contains a Lease resource which is used by the Policy controller to do leader election.  ArgoCD considers Leases to be runtime resources and will not deploy them.  This means that Linkerd will not work for users of ArgoCD.

We remove the policy-controller-write Lease resource from the Helm chart and instead have the policy controller create this resource at startup.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

Problem

Solution

Validation

Fixes #[GitHub issue ID]

DCO Sign off

Introduce Pull Request Template

GitHub's community guidelines recommend a pull request template, the repo was
lacking one.

Introduce a `PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` file.

Once merged, the
[Community profile checklist](https://github.com/linkerd/linkerd2/community)
should indicate the repo now provides a pull request template.

Fixes #3321

Signed-off-by: Jane Smith <jane.smith@example.com>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
